description: Well-known Hoffmann V60 technique — article text extraction
sourceText:
Source title:
The Ultimate V60 Technique — James Hoffmann

Source summary:
James Hoffmann shares his refined V60 pour-over technique, a method he has developed over years of experimentation. This recipe produces a clean, sweet, and balanced cup.

Structured page text:
The Ultimate V60 Technique
by James Hoffmann

What you need:
- 15g of medium-fine ground coffee
- 250g of water just off the boil (about 100°C / 212°F)
- Hario V60 dripper
- Paper filter, rinsed
- Gooseneck kettle

Method:

1. Place the V60 on your server, add the rinsed filter and coffee grounds.
2. Start your timer. Pour 50g of water to bloom the coffee. Give the V60 a gentle swirl to ensure all grounds are wet. Wait 45 seconds.
3. Starting at 0:45, pour slowly in concentric circles to 150g. This should take about 30 seconds.
4. At 1:15, pour again in concentric circles to 250g. This should take about 30 seconds.
5. Give the V60 a gentle swirl to knock grounds off the walls.
6. Wait for the drawdown to complete.

Total brew time should be between 3:00 and 3:30.

If the brew is too fast and tastes sour, grind finer. If it's too slow and tastes bitter, grind coarser.

Source URL:
https://www.jameshoffmann.co.uk/the-ultimate-v60-technique
